title: Rivian Q1 2023 Earnings Article
text: Rivian Automotive's stock rose 5.6% in after-hours trading following the release of its first-quarter 2023 results. The company exceeded Wall Street expectations on both revenue and earnings while reaffirming its annual production guidance. The company generated $661 million in Q1 revenue, surpassing the consensus estimate of $652 million. This represented a 596% increase year-over-year, though it remained flat compared to Q4 2022. The adjusted net loss was $1.17 billion, or $1.25 per share, a 13% narrowing from the loss of $1.43 per share in the year-ago period. Rivian produced 9,395 vehicles and delivered 7,946 units during the quarter. The sequential production decline occurred due to a scheduled shutdown of the commercial van line to integrate new motors and battery technology. The company ended the quarter with $11.8 billion in cash and equivalents, along with $2.72 billion in long-term debt. At the current burn rate, this liquidity could sustain operations for approximately 1.5 years. Rivian reaffirmed its target of producing 50,000 total vehicles this year, representing a 105% increase from 2022's output of 24,337 vehicles.
